column20: The proposed design ideas were effectively translated into the final outcome, with practical adjustments made to fit the space constraints. The designer maintained clear communication and was always receptive to our suggestions, ensuring our preferences were incorporated.
column21: While most things went well, there were a few areas where we felt let down. We had asked for additional sockets and wall lights in the hall, but were told it wasn’t possible due to the RCC wall. However, an external electrician later informed us that it could be done, which was a bit disappointing
column22: First of all, we found the overall cost to be quite high, and the project took much longer than expected. For a small 2BHK, 8 months felt excessive. Before booking, communication was quick and responsive, but after booking, we noticed significant delays in getting answers and updates. We often had to follow up repeatedly, which was frustrating. This is an area that definitely needs improvement—consistent communication and giving clients the same priority throughout the process would make a big difference.  That said, we are very happy with the final design. Our designer, Neha, was excellent—she understood our needs well, gave honest suggestions, and the outcome has turned out really nicely. Our project manager was also good, calm, and always willing to listen to our requests.  However, our experience with the rest of the team was disappointing.
